top6_cell_types: Respiratory ionocytes(734.1); Breast myoepithelial cells(424.5); Medullary thymic epithelial cells(412.8); Vascular endothelial cells(400.1); Fibroblasts(246.1); Basal keratinocytes(226.5)
interpretation: Fibroblasts / vascular endothelial / myoepithelial — no epithelial or tumor-cell signal. Classic stromal/ECM origin. Bulk-proteomic POSTN reflects tumor-associated stroma, not tumor cells → antibody/CAR targeting risks stromal-only engagement.

Single enriched cell type = proximal tubule cells (the ccRCC cell-of-origin's normal counterpart). KIM-1/HAVCR1 is a normal-tubule injury marker; on-tumor vs on-normal-kidney selectivity is intrinsically narrow.

top6_cell_types: Early spermatids(334.7); Gastric chief cells(287.2); Parietal cells(107.8); Foveolar cells(82.8); Mucous neck cells(80.2); Cholangiocytes(54.5)
interpretation: Not constitutively kidney-specific in NORMAL tissue (HPA shows spermatids/gastric). In ccRCC, CA9/CAIX is transcriptionally induced by VHL-loss→HIF stabilization and is a near-uniform tumor-cell surface antigen — established basis for girentuximab. Normal-tissue HPA cannot show the tumor-induced signal.

top6_cell_types: B-cells(75.9); Plasma cells(36.1); T-cells(32.5); Epididymal basal cells(25.8); Epididymal efferent duct ciliated cells(17.6); Fibroblasts(13.6)
interpretation: Normal expression restricted to activated B/T/plasma cells (immune). In ccRCC it is aberrantly expressed on tumor cells (basis for cusatuzumab / CD70 CAR-T). Immune-compartment normal expression is the key safety watch-item.

top6_cell_types: Extravillous trophoblasts(2420.5); Syncytiotrophoblasts(884.7); Neutrophils(849.6); Retinal pigment epithelial cells(660.8); Esophageal apical cells(585.4); Epicardial cells(379.8)
interpretation: MCT4 broadly expressed (trophoblast, neutrophil, RPE, epicardium high). Hypoxia-induced; not tumor-cell-restricted → selectivity is the central liability for MCT4 targeting.
